response:
The rental market in Miami, Florida, shows a wide range of prices. Single family homes have the highest mean price. Condos and apartments show lower median values. Standard deviation indicates high price variability. Outliers are common in luxury properties. Data filtering helps identify these extremes.

* * *

Segmenting by property type reveals distinct trends. Condos are more concentrated in downtown areas. Single family homes are spread across suburbs. Price distribution varies significantly. Median prices for apartments are lower than condos. Outliers in luxury condos skew the data.

* * *

Visualizing the data with Seaborn shows clear patterns. Boxplots highlight price outliers effectively. A bar chart compares median prices across types. Scatter plots reveal location-based trends. Outliers are marked for further analysis. These visuals support informed decision-making.
